categories.summary: The Discord channel discussion was dominated by a critical post from bangrrr2914 alleging financial irregularities in the Eliza Foundation's B2B announcement. The user claims that 750k CAD of holder assets were transferred to Secure Blockchain Dev Corp during an April 2026 funding round, with subsequent B2B development contracts merely circulating these same funds to create the appearance of organic enterprise revenue. The post also highlights that Sebastian Quinn Watson, a co-founder of the acquired Agentic Solutions, is a defendant in an active NY Southern District Court case against Eliza. The user demands transparency regarding the fund flow and warns the community about potential exit liquidity schemes. The only technical contribution came from richer_claude_51183, who announced the release of @omniology/plugin-omniology, a new plugin for ElizaOS agents that enables autonomous participation in skill-based contests on Solana mainnet. The plugin allows agents to compete in themed contests (Art, Story, Joke) every 88 seconds, with an LLM judge scoring entries and distributing real USDC prizes on-chain. The plugin has demonstrated a 72% win rate across 289 contests, earning $3.35 USDC with a 0.01 USDC entry fee per contest. Installation is straightforward via npm, and the system operates fully autonomously without human intervention. The remaining messages were brief greetings and promotional content without substantive technical discussion.

actionItems.description: Provide transparency documentation regarding 750k CAD fund flow between Eliza Foundation and Secure Blockchain Dev Corp
Investigate and clarify the relationship between B2B development contracts and the April 2026 funding round
